title: Scaling Depth vs. Width
concept: Scaling depth (adding layers) lets the model learn more complex abstractions; scaling width (hidden dimension size) increases representation capacity per token.

exercises.prompt: When we talk about 'depth' and 'width' in AI models, what are we referring to?

options.text: How many layers it has versus how large each layer is.
How fast it trains versus how much data it needs.
The physical size of the computer chip running it.

exercises.explain: In neural networks, depth refers to the number of sequential layers, while width refers to the size of those layers.

cards.title: Taller or Wider?
cards.body: When building giant AI models, researchers face a big choice: should they make the model taller by adding more layers, or wider by making the layers larger? Let's explore how this balance changes how AIs think!

cards.title: Depth vs. Width
cards.body: Scaling 'depth' means adding more layers to a model, allowing it to build complex, step-by-step reasoning. Scaling 'width' means increasing the size of the hidden layers (representation space), allowing the model to hold more details about each word at the same time.

cards.title: The Lego Tower
cards.body: Think of building a tower with Lego blocks. Scaling width is like making the tower wider—it lets you fit more details on each floor. Scaling depth is like building more floors high—it lets you build entirely new levels of structure, like adding a roof, balconies, and a spire!

cards.title: Storytellers in Action
cards.body: In a text model, a narrow but deep model might easily follow a complex detective story plot because it can stack many reasoning steps. A wide but shallow model might instantly remember thousands of unique vocabulary words but struggle with complex logical twists.

cards.title: The Width Trap
cards.body: Many believe that simply making a model wider is always better than making it deeper. In reality, a model that is too wide but has very few layers cannot learn complex, abstract concepts, no matter how many neurons you pack into those few layers!

cards.title: Visualizing Scale
cards.body: Let's compare what happens when we prioritize one scaling dimension over the other.

right.text: Expands layer size to capture a massive amount of detailed information at once.

left.text: Stacks many layers to build deep reasoning and extract abstract concepts step-by-step.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of scaling a model's width?

options.text: It increases the representation capacity per token.
It increases the number of sequential reasoning steps.
It makes the model run faster on simple devices.
It guarantees the model will never hallucinate.

exercises.explain: Width refers to the size of the hidden layers, which dictates how much information or how many details the model can hold for each token at a single level.

exercises.prompt: Scaling a model's depth means making its hidden layers wider.
exercises.explain: Depth is about the number of layers (sequential steps), whereas width is about the size of each layer (capacity per step).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of neural network dimensions.
exercises.sentence: Adding more sequential layers to a model increases its depth, helping it learn complex concepts.
exercises.explain: Adding more layers increases the model's depth, which allows it to learn more abstract concepts step-by-step.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements about scaling is incorrect and likely AI-generated?

options.text: Increasing depth allows a model to perform more steps of logical reasoning.
Increasing width allows a model to store more features per word.
Scaling depth makes the hidden layers wider, while scaling width adds more layers to the stack.

exercises.explain: The third option is generated by an AI because it falsely claims that depth makes the model wider, which is incorrect. The first two options accurately describe the roles of width and depth.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a new language model. If your goal is to balance how many features the model can hold at once versus how deeply it can reason about those features, which statement best guides your design?

options.text: Depth only memorizes facts, while width only processes grammar.
Width makes the network deeper, while depth makes it wider.
Depth helps learn complex abstractions, while width increases representation capacity.
Depth and width are identical and have the exact same effect on a model.

exercises.explain: Depth allows the model to process information through sequential steps to extract complex features, while width expands the model's capacity to represent many different features at a single step. Balancing both is the key to efficient scaling!
